candidates.title: Remote Lightroom Editors for Corporate Events
candidates.brief: Orange Photography is looking to add experienced remote Lightroom editors to our post-production roster for corporate event photography projects. We are a national photography and video agency specializing in conferences, corporate events, meetings, headshot activations, executive portraits, and multi-city event programs. Our team works with principal photographers and vetted associates across the U.S., supporting clients who expect consistent quality, fast turnaround, and a professional editing workflow. This role is ideal for editors who are highly efficient in Lightroom and comfortable working through large event galleries with speed, consistency, and attention to detail. **About the Role** Remote Lightroom editors will work with image sets photographed by our principal photographers and event teams. The primary work includes culling, basic color correction, exposure balancing, white

candidates.title: Eye-Catching Social Post Graphics
candidates.tools: Photoshop · Illustrator — for layered/retouched assets and multi-page layout
candidates.brief: I need a set of fresh, visually engaging post graphics designed for my social-media channels. The goal is to stop users mid-scroll with layouts that feel on-brand, instantly communicate the message, and look great on both mobile and desktop feeds. Here is what I have in mind: • Format: square (1080×1080) and portrait (1080×1350) versions for each design, ready to upload to Instagram, Facebook, and LinkedIn. • Quantity: an initial batch of 5 posts so I can review style, followed by additional rounds once we lock in the look. • Deliverables: layered source files (PSD or AI) plus web-ready PNG/JPG exports. My brand palette and copy for each post will be provided; you help with creative layout, typography, iconography or simple illustrations as needed. Turnaround on the first draft should be quick, and I only release payment when the designs meet the agreed concept, clarity, and visual polis

candidates.title: Engaging Historical YouTube Thumbnails
candidates.tools: Photoshop · Illustrator — for layered/retouched assets and multi-page layout
candidates.brief: I’m preparing a new batch of YouTube videos and the historical series is going live first, so I need an eye-catching thumbnail (or a small set, depending on your concept) that instantly signals “history” to viewers while still looking fresh in the feed. Core concept • The thumbnail must feature recognisable historical figures—portraits, busts, silhouettes, or any creative treatment that fits the topic. • Colour grading, typography and layout should evoke a sense of the past without feeling dusty; think cinematic teasers rather than textbook covers. Secondary uses Once this historical design language is locked in, I’ll adapt the same visual energy to upcoming horror, vlog and story episodes, so design choices should be versatile enough for future tweaks. What I need from you • A polished thumbnail delivered in 1280 × 720 px (max 2 MB, .PNG & .PSD). • A quick preview mock-up on a typical Y

candidates.title: Artistic Photo & Video Enhancements -- 2
candidates.tools: Photoshop · After Effects · Premiere Pro — for layered/retouched assets and video/motion editing
candidates.brief: I have a batch of photos—portraits, candid event shots, and venue images—that all need to be elevated with creative special effects. The core of the job is applying striking artistic filters to each image so they look cohesive in a final album and ready for both social media and large-format printing. While artistic filters are the main request, you’re welcome to suggest complementary touches such as subtle color tweaks or a background swap whenever it will amplify the effect; I’m open to any style direction that fits the mood of a lively celebration. Alongside the photos, one short clip from the event also needs visual decoration: tasteful overlays, animated titles, or other light motion graphics that mirror the photographic treatment so the whole package feels seamless. Deliverables • 30 retouched images in high-resolution JPG and layered PSD/PNG versions • 1 decorated video clip (≈60

candidates.title: Traditional Corporate Headshot Session Needed
candidates.tools: Photoshop · After Effects — for layered/retouched assets
candidates.brief: I’m scheduling a straightforward corporate head-shot and would like a photographer who can handle a classic, formal look. I will come to your location—whether that is your studio or another space you regularly shoot in—so there is no need for an on-site setup at my office. Style & background • Traditional / formal framing and lighting • Neutral light-grey backdrop (no textured or patterned alternatives) Deliverables • 3–5 high-resolution, fully retouched images suitable for LinkedIn, company website and print • Both colour and clean black-and-white versions • Turnaround within a week of the shoot Please let me know your available dates, the type of lighting rig you prefer for this setup, and any prep guidelines I should follow before arriving.
